title: ICT Cloud & Security Engineer
description: PFH Technology are currently looking for an **ICT Cloud \& Security Engineer** to join our growing team. **Location:** Dublin 1, hybrid **Contract length** : 12\-months (with expected extensions) **Responsibilities:** The ideal candidate will have at least 10\-years’ experience in the following: * Configure and manage Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for identity governance, conditional access, and MFA. * Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and role\-based access controls. * Deploy and manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, application deployment, and endpoint configuration. * Configure \& manage Microsoft Defender XDR solutions (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365 etc). * Monitor and respond to security alerts using Microsoft Sentinel and other SIEM tools. * Apply best practices for securing Azure resources, including network security groups, firewalls, and encryption. * Implement Zero Trust principles across identity, devices, and applications. * Lead investigations for security incidents and provide remediation strategies. * Maintain detailed documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures. * Must be able to show a high degree of awareness of cybersecurity best practices and concepts including phishing awareness, Identity and Access Management, Multifactor Authentication, Zero Trust Security Model, Purview specially Information Protection * Must have demonstrated experience of threat evaluation and security incident response * Must have exposure to and be able to demonstrate experience using an ITIL aligned Service desk and associated principles such as Incident and Service request management, Problem Management, Change Management, continual Service improvement etc. * Must be able to demonstrate excellent support \& troubleshooting skills for Level 3 Incident Management aligned with ITIL best practices (Incident management, Service Request and Problem Management). **Duties:** * Be willing and capable to work well within a team environment * Be able to clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner demonstrating a customer service mind\-set * Provide third line support for ICT Operations and other ICT Teams * Liaise with other ICT technical team specialists within the tenderer’s organisation to complete projects, (the response should clearly detail the areas where the tenderer has technical expertise). * Be able to follow Incident Management Process. * Create and update our Knowledge Management system, SOP’s, and Runbooks. * To contribute effectively to organisational and team processes and adhere to policies including but not limited to Data Protection and Information Security * Be responsible for ensuring the IT systems and applications are maintained to best practice. * Recognise system deficiencies and suggest effective solutions while complying with company policies and procedures. * Research current best practice technology solutions and translate them into our environment * To participate in Change Advisory Board (CAB) and Problem Management meetings and to share information with the support teams. * Be responsible for resolution of all tickets within agreed KPI timelines. * Carry out any other duties as required. **Education:** Must hold \- Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate AZ\-500 Must hold at least one of the following but all three would be advantageous: * Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate SC\-200 * Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator Associate SC\-300 * Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate MD\-102 PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to ensuring that individuals with disabilities or access needs are provided with reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, to perform essential job functions, and to access the full benefits of employment. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage.

title: IT Governance & Compliance Analyst (ISO27001)
description: IT Governance \& Compliance Analyst (ISO27001\) * Dublin 1 (Hybrid – 2 days onsite) * 12\-Month FTC **The Opportunity** We’re partnering with a leading organisation in the education and research sector to hire an IT Governance \& Compliance Analyst on a 12\-month contract. This is a key hire supporting a major ISO27001 certification programme, working at the intersection of IT, security, and governance. This role is ideal for someone with a technical background (e.g. IT Manager, Systems Administrator, Application Support) who has been involved in audits or compliance initiatives and wants to move further into GRC. **What You’ll Be Doing** You’ll act as the link between technical teams and the GRC function, helping drive ISO readiness by: * Working closely with IT, service, and technical teams to gather key information * Translating technical processes into clear, structured documentation * Supporting ISO27001 compliance and audit preparation * Mapping existing processes and controls to ISO requirements * Collecting and organising audit evidence and documentation * Ensuring consistency and quality across operational documentation * Supporting broader regulatory and compliance initiatives (incl. NIS2 readiness) **What We’re Looking For** * Background in IT / Technical roles (e.g. Sys Admin, IT Support, IT Manager, Applications) * Exposure to ISO27001, audits, or compliance processes * Ability to communicate confidently with technical stakeholders * Strong documentation and organisational skills * Comfortable asking questions, gathering information, and driving progress * Understanding of IT systems, infrastructure, or service environments * Familiarity with tools like SharePoint, reporting tools, or ticketing systems **Why Apply?** * Strong work\-life balance and flexible hybrid model * Opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in a major ISO27001 programme * Work across a broad technical environment with high\-impact stakeholders * Excellent stepping stone into a long\-term GRC / Information Security career Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Programme, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise, while making real contributions to Autodesk’s manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: International Freight Forwarding Logistics Specialist – Expo & Event
description: **Company Description** Interflow Logistics is a specialist international freight forwarder focused on the delivery of **high‑stakes, time‑sensitive shipments for live events worldwide** . We work behind the scenes on some of the world’s most demanding projects, where accuracy, timing and coordination are critical to success. Our core areas of expertise include: * **Trade Shows \& Exhibitions** – Managing international transport, customs clearance and on‑site logistics for global events * **Live Events** – Delivering logistics solutions for large‑scale corporate and public events * **Sports Logistics** – Handling specialist transport and on‑site support for professional sports bodies around the world From navigating complex customs regulations to providing hands‑on support on the ground, Interflow is trusted to deliver where failure is not an option. **Role Description** We’re looking to bring someone new into Interflow who is **early in their career, eager to learn, and genuinely interested in international events** . The **International Freight Forwarding Logistics Specialist – Expo \& Event** will support the planning and coordination of logistics services for exhibitions and live events. This is a **hands‑on, fast‑paced role** , working closely with experienced colleagues, international partners and clients. This is **not a standard 9–5 logistics position** . Live events are dynamic by nature, and flexibility, curiosity and calm problem‑solving are essential. This is a full‑time, on‑site role based in **Dublin** . **Candidates must be currently living in Ireland and available to start immediately. Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.** **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinating international freight forwarding for exhibitions and live events * Supporting export and import processes, customs clearance and compliance * Liaising with clients, overseas agents, airlines and transport providers * Tracking shipments and resolving issues in real time * Supporting on‑site logistics during event delivery where required * Providing a high level of service in a deadline‑driven environment **Who This Role Is Suited To** This role is ideal for: * A **recent graduate** or someone at an **early stage of their logistics career** * Someone interested in **events, exhibitions or international projects** * A person who enjoys problem‑solving and variety rather than routine work **Salary \& Benefits** * **Competitive entry‑level salary** in line with experience and potential * Typical starting range: **€40,000 – €45,000 gross per annum** * Clear opportunity for **salary progression** as experience and responsibility increase * 22 days annual leave plus public holidays * Pension scheme after qualifying period * Exposure to international events and travel opportunities over time This role is designed as a development position, with increased responsibility and remuneration as experience is gained. Why Join Interflow At Interflow, you won’t be managing routine shipments you’ll be contributing to the successful delivery of international exhibitions, live events and sports projects where timing is critical and no two days are the same. This role offers hands‑on learning, close collaboration with experienced colleagues, and the opportunity to build a long‑term career in specialist event logistics within a fast‑paced, professional environment.

title: Investment Associate
description: Cairn is recruiting for an Investment Associate to join our team. **Responsibilities:** * Support underwriting of acquisitions and existing development opportunities including financial modelling, investment memos, deal structuring and due diligence. * Providing accurate financial modelling and scenario planning to enable effective decision making across the portfolio. * Work cross functionally in compiling, preparing and finalising development appraisals and modelling to support key decision making. * Support the Investment Team in gateway review stages, ensuring that financial, commercial, and design teams are aligned before presentation to senior leadership team members. * Support the long‑term land pipeline planning through site‑by‑site optimisation \& exit strategy * Assist in preparing weekly functional presentations for senior leadership team. * Conduct market research including researching market trends, competitor positioning, and policy changes influencing acquisition strategy and broader portfolio positioning. **Requirements:** * Degree or relevant experience in real estate, finance, economics etc. * Strong financial modelling capability. * Keen interest in development. * 3\-5 years’ experience. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * Strong presentation skills. * Strong problem solving and attention to detail\-orientation. * Highly commercial, proactive and comfortable working in an entrepreneurial environment. **Working with Cairn Homes** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join Cairn which include * Competitive salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Full health insurance with Irish Life health * Annual salary review * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Free Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * Employee Assistance Programme with VHI * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture Committee * Talent Development Programmes * Approved Profit Sharing Scheme (APSS) The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.

title: Investment Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. An opportunity has arisen for an **Investment Associate** within the Private Clients department at Davy. **About The Role** * Working closely with an Associate Director/Director: * Assist in the creation and execution of relationship plans for each client relating to the implementation of their financial plan, adherence with service standards which lead to the development of long\-term client relationships; * Identifying revenue potential and opportunities to cross\-sell Davy services; * Review existing client relationships, and in line with Investment Strategy, analyse whether the relationships are categorised correctly and therefore managed appropriately; * Execute a engagement plan for each advisory relationship to ensure they receive proactive, relevant and appropriate contact, investment ideas and recommendations and structured portfolio reviews; * Managing MI relating to client relationships; * Deal promptly and efficiently with client queries; * Ensure that all activities and duties are carried out in full compliance with regulatory requirements, Davy's Risk Management Framework and internal Policies and Policy Standards; * Be recognised as an expert and influential contact for Davy's services and in relation to client queries; * Ability to network with peers at industry events. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role **Requirements** **About you** * Degree or Masters in Business related area; * 2\-3 years industry experience; * QFA qualification; * A high degree of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word and PowerPoint; * Proficient in CRM; * A commitment to further education and continuous learning; * Demonstrable interest and understanding of investments; * A strong client focus, to ensure effective service delivery; * Strong communication and presentation skills; * Excellent attention to detail; * Excellent organisational skills; * An ability to prioritise own workload. **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. * Health and Wellbeing Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. * Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. * Growth and Development Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. * Positive Workplace Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. * Values and Culture Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group. *The position set out above is defined as a "Controlled Function (CF)" role, namely CF3, CF4, CF10\.under Section 20 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and Schedule 1 of the Regulations (S.I. No. 437 of 2011\) as amended. In accordance with Section 21 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and the Fitness and Probity Standards 2014, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Fitness \& Probity requirements, applicable to this position. The role is also subject to the Minimum Competency Code and therefore there is a requirement to hold, or be willing to take the APA (Regulation \& Investment) or (Pensions) or Registered Stockbroker or QFA exams.*

title: Ireland Residents - Survey Participants - Bray, Ireland
description: **Survey Participants \- Ireland** ---------------------------------- #### ***About Prolific*** Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world. Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills. #### ***The role*** We are looking for Study Participants from all professional backgrounds across **Ireland** to take part in academic and applied research studies for global businesses and educational faculties. Whether you are just starting your career or are a seasoned expert, your perspective is valuable. We are actively seeking a diverse range of educational backgrounds, including: * No High School Diploma / High School Graduates * Trade School \& Vocational Professionals (MBO) * Undergraduate \& Master's Degree Holders (HBO/WO) * PhD Candidates \& Doctoral Researchers If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10\- to 15\-minute screening to assess your suitability. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to contribute to a wide range of projects. Compensation: Researchers looking for your profile tend to **pay up to $18/hr** per study completed. While many tasks are short, you should be prepared for studies that **require up to one hour of uninterrupted work**. #### ***What you'll be doing in the role*** As a participant, you will contribute to high\-quality research outcomes by responding thoughtfully to various tasks, including: * AI Training \& Evaluation: helping refine Large Language Models by ranking responses or providing creative prompts. * Behavioral Experiments: participating in decision\-making tasks and cognitive games. * User Research: providing feedback on new software, apps, or product concepts. * Academic Studies: assisting university researchers in fields like psychology, economics, and sociology. * Interviews: engaging in one\-on\-one deep dives regarding your professional expertise or lived experience. * Surveys \& Questionnaires: sharing your opinions on current events, consumer habits, or workplace culture. #### ***What you'll bring*** * Diverse Perspective: a track record of relevant experience, knowledge, or lived context (from entry\-level roles to executive or academic positions). * Attention to Detail: the ability to remain focused and provide high\-quality data for the duration of a study. * Technical Setup: a reliable, fast internet connection and access to a desktop or laptop computer. * Financial Readiness: a PayPal account to receive payments and a willingness to self\-declare earnings as a self\-employed participant. #### ***Why Join Prolific?*** Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to contribute to meaningful research used by universities, researchers, and global organizations. Once you pass our screening, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home. We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and organizations with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high\-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data helps researchers answer important questions, improve products and services, and advance knowledge across disciplines. We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity. ### ***Links \& Privacy*** Website Youtube ### ***Privacy Statement*** By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific's use of your personal information.

title: Junior Security Operations Engineer
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* **About The Role** We’re looking for a Junior Security Operations Engineer who is AI\-Forward to help scale and modernize our SecOps program. This is a hands\-on, builder role for someone who will design and ship the security tooling that powers our triage, investigations, and response workflows. You’ll report to the Technical Operations Director and work alongside our GRC lead to improve our vulnerability intake, threat response, darkweb posture, and internal security tooling. A core part of this role is building AI\-assisted security tooling: triage agents that pre\-classify bug bounty reports, investigation copilots that pull context from logs and SIEM data, response workflows that draft remediation steps and track them to closure. You’ll spend as much time wiring up that tooling as you will reproducing vulnerabilities and working incidents. This role suits someone who thrives in a lean, high\-impact environment, has strong opinions on where humans add value versus where tooling should take over, and wants to shape how a modern security team operates. **What You’ll Do** **Triage \& Vulnerability Management** * Review incoming vulnerability reports from our bug bounty intake; reproduce and document valid issues for engineering teams. * Build the tooling that improves signal\-to\-noise: automate duplicate detection, spam filtering, and abuse flagging so humans only touch what’s worth touching. * Act on DAST findings: coordinate remediation, verify fixes, and re\-test. * Track remediation timelines for critical vulnerabilities and keep stakeholders honest on SLAs. **Threat Response \& Monitoring** * Monitor and respond to EDR and cloud security alerts; investigate, contain, and document. * Analyze darkweb findings and credential exposures; shape our darkweb monitoring practices and tooling over time. * Improve detection coverage: tune noisy rules, close gaps, and enrich alerts with context so responders spend time on decisions, not data gathering. * Help configure and tune DLP, SIEM, and AI security tooling. **Security Tooling (core to this role)** * Build AI\-assisted triage tooling that pre\-classifies bug bounty reports, flags duplicates, and routes real issues to the right engineering team with reproduction context attached. * Build investigation tooling: LLM\-backed copilots and Slack bots that pull alert context, correlate signals across sources, and surface what actually matters. * Build response tooling: workflows that draft remediation steps, generate evidence artifacts, and track issues to closure across the teams that own them. * Evaluate emerging AI security tooling and bring what’s genuinely useful into the stack; retire what isn’t. * Apply a security\-minded lens to our own AI usage: prompt injection, data exfiltration, model misuse, and the guardrails that go around them. **Compliance \& Cross\-Functional** * Support audit evidence collection for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. * Partner with ITOps to verify patches, endpoint coverage, and access hygiene. **You May Be a Good Fit If You Have** * Previous experience in a SecOps, Security Analyst, or Threat Response role. * Proven ability to understand and reproduce technical vulnerabilities. You can read a bug report and tell whether it’s real. * Experience with bug bounty triage (HackerOne, Bugcrowd, or similar). * Hands\-on exposure to SIEM, EDR, and DLP tools in production environments. * A genuine, demonstrated interest in applying AI/LLMs to security work: side projects, internal tooling, prompt engineering, agent frameworks, anything that shows you’ve actually built with this stuff. * Scripting and automation skills in Python, Bash, or similar. You reach for code before you reach for a checklist. * Comfort working autonomously across time zones and asynchronously. * Strong written communication. You can turn a messy investigation into a clear, defensible writeup. **Bonus Points For** * Experience building LLM agents, RAG pipelines, or Slack bots backed by OpenAI / Anthropic / open\-source models. * Detection engineering experience (Sigma rules, custom Falcon queries, Grafana/Loki dashboards). * Cloud security depth in AWS or GCP (IAM, workload posture, cloud\-native logging). * Prior work in regulated environments (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS) from the practitioner side. * Exposure to AI/ML security concerns such as prompt injection, model evals, and data leakage through LLMs. * Public contributions: blog posts, CVEs, open\-source tools, CTF writeups. **Engagement \& Logistics** * Full\-time contract. * Remote\-first and async\-friendly. We have hubs in San Francisco, Denver, Dublin, and Amsterdam. If you’re near one, we’d love to see you in the office regularly; if you’re elsewhere in the world, that works too. * Potential to extend or convert based on fit. * Reports to the Technical Operations Director; works closely with the GRC lead and IT Operations. **How We Work** We’re a lean, high\-trust team. We value people who ship, who can operate independently, and who treat security as an engineering problem rather than a checklist. If you’re someone who sees a repetitive task and immediately thinks “this should be a script, or better yet, an agent,” you’ll fit in here. **To Apply** Tell us about a time you used AI, automation, or custom tooling to meaningfully change how a security workflow ran. What was manual before, what it looked like after, and what you learned. Links to code, writeups, or demos are welcome.

To ensure that we have the right structures in place to support the achievement of our goals we are currently recruiting for a **Learning \& Development Executive** . We wish to recruit an enthusiastic, motivated and adaptable person to join the Firm’s HR \& People Development team. The function of this role is to support the Legal and Professional Development team in delivering development programmes, enabling our internal talent to achieve their full potential. The role requires experience in the delivery of training and supporting performance management. Based in our Dublin office, the successful candidate will report to the Director of Legal and Professional Development and work closely with the wider HR and People Development team. Applications for appointment on a full\-time basis or a 4 day week basis will be considered. The Learning \& Development Executive will have responsibility for: * Manage and coordinate the delivery of MF Advance, our in\-house training programme delivered to trainee solicitors as part of the Law Society of Ireland’s training requirements. * Liaise, as required, with the Law Society, MF Advance subject leads and/or the external examiners on the MF Advance curriculum design, timetabling, trainee assessments and related issues. * Manage our established training programmes. * Create and develop further programmes for trainees and junior associates. * Support the trainee and associate performance review process. * Analyse individual personal development plans, focusing on the provision of meaningful solutions, including the design, delivery and evaluation of all training provided. * Provider management (sourcing, selecting, reviewing and negotiating costs). * Ensure that cost effective training is planned and delivered making the best use of internal/external resources available. * Provide support and feedback to employees as they engage in career planning and development work. * Support the annual early careers programmes. **Requirements:** The successful candidate will have: Education: * A third level qualification \- Training / Law/HR/Business/ Psychology or similar. Professional Experience: * The ideal candidate will have in the region of 3\-5 years' experience in a Learning \& Development role. * Experience working with management teams and building strong credible relationships. * Demonstrate the ability to work with and effectively communicate to all levels within an organisation and to deliver on learning targets in a cost effective and timely manner. * Previous experience within a professional services firm or similar would be advantageous. Knowledge \& Expertise: * Experience in design and delivery of workshops and other training formats; * Ability to engage with senior stakeholders to scope, design and implement group learning events; * Experience of exploring and assessing development needs with individuals; * Experience of carrying out observation and giving feedback; * Qualified to use psychometric tools would be advantageous. Skills: * Strong interpersonal, facilitation and mentoring skills are essential; * Demonstrate strong written and oral communication skills; * A high degree of discretion; * A positive attitude and a collaborative approach to working; * Digitally literate, with an interest in emerging technologies, including Generative AI. **Our Offer** Competitive salary Hybrid working options Pension contributions Life assurance and income protection cover Relocation assistance (for those joining from overseas) A great working environment A lively sports and social club **Applications** To apply for this role, please email careers@mccannfitzgerald.com with your CV and, for a confidential discussion or for more information, please contact Eimear Power (Recruitment Manager) on \+353 1 607 12 92

title: Linux Kernel Engineer
description: **Summary** At Apple, we craft experiences that have transformed entire industries. The diversity of our people and ideas fuels innovation in everything we do. Imagine what you could build here. Join our Linux Kernel Engineering team and play a central role in the reliability, performance, and evolution of Apple’s platforms. As a Linux Kernel Engineer, you will own the kernel lifecycle end to end, from CI infrastructure and automated validation to driver enablement, debugging, and production\-quality releases. You will partner closely with platform, infrastructure, and SRE teams to ensure every kernel we ship is stable, observable, and ready for real world workloads at scale. **Description** In this role, you will design, build, and operate CI and validation pipelines that compile, test, and release Linux kernels across multiple hardware platforms and Linux distributions. You will lead triage of kernel regressions, investigate crashes and performance issues, drive fixes to resolution, and qualify new hardware at the kernel layer. You will collaborate with engineers across Apple to port, maintain, and upstream device drivers, shepherd kernel changes into production, and continuously raise the bar on kernel quality, automation, and release discipline. Your work will have direct impact on the stability, performance, and debuggability of systems that power critical Apple services. If you enjoy deep kernel debugging, building robust CI systems, and owning kernel releases from development through production, we would like to hear from you. **Minimum Qualifications** * Experience developing or maintaining the Linux kernel or low\-level system software (e.g., kernel modules, device drivers, or core OS components). * Proven ability to debug kernel level issues (panics, hangs, performance regressions) using tools such as perf, ftrace, bpftrace, crash, gdb, or similar. * Deep understanding of kernel tuning for stability and performance across many subsystem domains. * Hands\-on experience developing CI/CD systems integrating operating system components and managing kernel releases. * Strong programming skills in C, plus proficiency in at least one of Rust, Go, Python, or Bash. * Solid understanding of operating system fundamentals: processes, memory management, filesystems, networking, scheduling, interrupts, and hardware/driver interactions. * Experience working in a RHEL based Linux environment, including RPM packaging and the RHEL kernel back port and release process. * Strong collaboration and communication skills; comfortable working with cross\-functional teams (SRE, platform, hardware, security). **Preferred Qualifications** * Proven track record of upstream contributions to the Linux kernel project or similar upstreams. * Bachelor of Computer Science or equivalent skills and work experience.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Mobile QA Architect
description: **About Us** Gen is a global company dedicated to powering Digital Freedom through its trusted consumer brands including Norton, Avast, LifeLock, MoneyLion and more. Our combined heritage is rooted in financial empowerment and cyber safety for the first digital generations, and today we deliver award\-winning cybersecurity, online privacy, identity protection and financial wellness solutions to nearly 500 million users in more than 150 countries. Together, we share a collective passion and vision to protect consumers and help them grow, manage and secure their digital and financial lives. We’re always looking for smart, fearless and high\-impact talent who see AI as a teammate – leveraging it to move faster and deliver meaningful results. When you’re part of Gen, you’ll have the flexibility, tools and support to do your best work and grow your career – from flexible working options and time off to competitive pay, benefits and well\-being programs. At Gen, we are scrappy and relentlessly customer driven. We create room for healthy debate, experimentation and continuous learning, and we seek out people with different experiences, identities and ideas to join our team. You’ll work with people who back each other, respect each other and understand that our differences are a competitive advantage. If this sounds like you, we’d love you to be part of Gen. **About The Role** We are pioneering an AI\-first development culture. Every aspect of our product development lifecycle is infused with AI adoption—from ideation and design to coding, testing, and deployment. Our mission is to lead the industry in AI\-powered mobile innovation. Quality assurance represents a critical leverage point for AI\-driven productivity in mobile engineering. In this role, you will help transform QA into a strategic, AI\-enabled function that drives both velocity and quality across the mobile engineering organization. **In This Role, You Will** * Build and mentor a world\-class mobile QA engineering team, ensuring high\-impact Android and iOS applications meet the highest quality standards for millions of users worldwide. * Champion AI\-first testing practices, leveraging tools such as GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, and generative testing frameworks to automate test creation, optimize test coverage, accelerate bug detection, and elevate product quality. * Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to define and drive a comprehensive quality assurance strategy for mobile products. * Lead the evolution of test automation frameworks and infrastructure, leveraging AI to create intelligent, maintainable, and scalable test suites. * Foster a quality\-first mindset across the mobile engineering organization, positioning QA as a strategic partner rather than a bottleneck. * Remove blockers, set clear priorities, and enable your team to deliver high\-quality outcomes in a fast\-paced, global environment. **About You** * You are an inspiring leader who attracts and develops top talent, leads by example, communicates clearly, and builds trust through authenticity and competence * You have an AI\-native mindset and believe in AI’s transformative potential for quality assurance as a force multiplier, not a replacement * You bring deep technical expertise in mobile QA across Android and/or iOS, including understanding of device fragmentation, OS\-specific challenges, and robust test strategy design * You are a hands\-on manager who stays connected to the work, writes test code, and understands the day\-to\-day realities of your team * You have experience with programming languages such as Java, Kotlin, Swift, or Python * You are curious and continuously learning, especially in AI tooling for QA, mobile testing innovations, and team dynamics * You thrive in cross\-functional collaboration and build strong relationships with product, engineering, design, and DevOps teams * You are a strategic quality builder who shapes team culture, establishes meaningful quality metrics, and drives continuous improvement **What's Next** If you're excited about this opportunity, we’d love to hear from you! The hiring process consists of two technical interview rounds and one managerial interview. Throughout the process, you will have the opportunity to meet the hiring manager and future team members.

title: Nap OS Career Simulator (Un-paid)
description: **Company Description** Napblog Limited, headquartered in Dublin, specializes in product development and go\-to\-market solutions to bridge skills, technology, and employment outcomes. Its flagship product, Nap OS, is a career and recruitment operating system built from over 18 months of global research, enabling institutions and professionals to measure capability based on outcomes rather than credentials. The company also provides AI adoption and go\-to\-market services, designing and deploying AI agents, automation systems, and market launch strategies for clients across Europe. With a focus on real\-world applications, Napblog Limited validates its frameworks and services publicly through extensive research and insights. **Role Description** The Nap OS Career Simulator is a remote role focused on delivering customized career counseling and professional development support. This role involves engaging with users to understand and enhance their career journeys, providing personalized career development plans, and analyzing data to identify areas of improvement. Daily tasks include delivering exceptional customer service, facilitating interactive career simulations, and maintaining effective communication to ensure high levels of user satisfaction and success. The position is execution\-focused and aligned with Napblog's mission to deliver measurable career outcomes. **Qualifications** * Strong Career Counseling and Career Development skills, with the ability to provide actionable insights and personalized guidance * Excellent Communication and Customer Service skills, ensuring positive engagement and user satisfaction * Proficient Analytical Skills for assessing user data and identifying key actionable areas * Experience in tech\-enabled career tools or employment systems is an advantage * Ability to work independently in a remote environment, with strong time management skills * Bachelor's degree or relevant experience in career development, counseling, or human resources is preferred **Napblog Limited** builds **Nap OS** — The Career Accelerator \& Employability Operating System designed to convert ambition into verified career outcomes. Traditional internships often reward attendance, passive learning, and vague experience claims. Modern hiring demands more. Employers increasingly want proof of capability, execution discipline, communication skills, and real\-world outputs. Nap OS was built to solve this gap. Instead of asking people to “gain experience” without structure, Nap OS provides a guided accelerator where participants build measurable employability evidence through execution\-based learning, simulation projects, portfolio creation, and career coaching. This is **not a traditional internship.** This is a **paid career accelerator programme** for serious individuals who want structured progress, accountability, and faster career readiness. **Nap OS Internship Simulator (Career Accelerator Programme)** Remote \| Structured Monthly Programme \| €99 per month The Nap OS Internship Simulator is a guided employability accelerator for students, graduates, immigrants, and career changers who want to become job\-ready through real execution. Participants do not pay for a job. They invest in a structured system that helps them: • build an interview\-ready portfolio • gain role\-based simulated experience • develop practical outputs • improve confidence and clarity • receive guidance and accountability • increase employability signal to recruiters Unlike passive online courses, Nap OS focuses on execution, evidence, and momentum. All selected participants begin with a **14\-Day Execution Sprint** inside Nap OS. This sprint is designed to measure seriousness, consistency, ownership, and ability to follow through. During the sprint, participants may complete tasks such as: • research challenges • market analysis • content creation • workflow building • portfolio improvements • AI productivity tasks • business case exercises • documentation assignments Every completed activity contributes toward a growing portfolio of proof\-of\-work. **Depending on your career direction, outputs may include:** • Marketing campaign plans • SEO audits • Content assets • Business reports • Recruiter\-ready CV improvements • AI workflow automations • Data analysis summaries • Operations dashboards • Case studies • LinkedIn positioning assets **As a Nap OS member (€99/month), participants receive:** • Structured monthly roadmap • Guided internship simulation projects • Portfolio development support • Career accountability system • Proof\-of\-work evidence building • Employability\-focused feedback • Certification upon completion • Priority access to future Napblog opportunities • Potential referrals for strong performers • Entry into the Nap OS talent ecosystem **This programme is best suited for people who are tired of waiting and ready to build.** You may be a: • Student needing experience • Graduate lacking interviews • Immigrant seeking local market readiness • Career changer entering a new field • Ambitious self\-starter wanting momentum Strong candidates demonstrate: • Ownership mindset • Consistency • Curiosity • Professional communication • Coachability • Willingness to execute weekly **This is not employment and not a guaranteed placement.** This is a premium career acceleration service designed to help participants become more employable through structured action. Those seeking passive certificates, guaranteed jobs, or resume\-only validation may not be a fit. Those ready to build real credibility through work will benefit most. **Many talented people stay stuck because they lack:** • structure • proof of skills • accountability • visibility • market direction Nap OS helps solve all five.

title: Operations Analyst
description: **About Financefair** At Financefair, we're not just another Fintech company. We're a dedicated team of professionals committed to providing innovative financial solutions that propel businesses forward. Specialising in funding the growth of Irish and UK SMEs, we pride ourselves on our ability to foster success through innovative financial solutions. Since our beginning in 2015, we have been obsessed about providing the very best and most innovative solutions, and a best\-in\-class customer experience, to become the finance partner of choice. Over the last 10 years, Financefair has funded over €2\.0 billion for a wide range of clients across all sectors. We are in a period of rapid growth, launching new technology, expanding our team and growing our client base. **The Role** As an Operations Analyst, you will work in a dynamic team gaining an in\-depth exposure to the Financefair Platform, and hands\-on experience of dealing with scaling SMEs and large institutional credit funds. You will be given the opportunity and the autonomy to affect change and growth within a FinTech company who is striving to be the market leader in innovation, technology, credit, risk, and operational excellence within the receivables financing industry. The role will provide you with direct access to the Financefair leadership team and our inhouse Tech team, which will provide a rich learning experience for the successful candidate. The position is based at the company’s head office in Belfield Office Park, Clonskeagh, Dublin with 4 days (Mon to Thu) in the office, with Friday’s working remote. **Key Responsibilities** Operations Responsibilities: * Funder relationship management \- daily funder management, funder allocations and new funder onboarding * Daily cash and asset management * Daily fund reconciliation and allocation management * Drawdown management * Foreign Exchange management * Data analysis and reporting * Process automation and improvement initiatives * Drive change via technology and automation of processes via an agile methodology * Perform ongoing portfolio risk monitoring and identify emerging credit or operational risks **Technology And Risk Innovation Responsibilities** * Automating risk and operational workflows to improve control effectiveness and efficiency * Implementing technology\-driven solutions to enhance risk visibility and decision\-making * Supporting digital transformation initiatives within risk and operations function **The Ideal Candidate** * 3rd level degree in business, finance, or similar. * Experience in operations within the financial services or FinTech sector is highly desirable * Will have strong time management skills and have an ability to be flexible, prioritise their work, work to deadlines and ensure queries resolved in a timely manner * Highly numeric with a razor\-sharp attention to detail is critical * Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written communication and presentational skills with an ability to handle queries in a professional and timely manner * Show a high level of commitment, professional work ethic and is ambitious * Demonstrated ability to collaborate across teams, including Tech, Risk, and Credit, to achieve shared goals * Self\-motivated and able to use own initiative * Foster a culture of operational excellence * An ability to question and improve processes and use software where necessary to do things more efficiently * Proficient in Microsoft Office and experience of working with Microsoft Excel will be a distinct advantage * Relevant certifications (e.g., Lean Six Sigma, CFA, ACCA or similar) are a distinct advantage * Interested in innovation and tech

title: PET CT Clinical Specialist
description: Alliance Medical currently have an exciting opportunity with an excellent remuneration package available for a PET\-CT Clinical Specialist to join our centre in Co Dublin! The PET\-CT Clinical Specialist will lead the PET\-CT unit, focusing on service delivery, quality assurance, and staff management. This role involves a combination of expert clinical duties, such as ensuring the correct protocols are followed and manage the day\-to\-day operations of the PET\-CT service, and leadership responsibilities, like training staff and supervising service delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * In conjunction with the PET\-CT Clinical and Administrative staff, provide a safe \& efficient PET\-CT service, while adhering to all legislative regulations and company policies. * Accountable for the quality of the service provided, including customer service \& technical aspects. * Developing and implementing operational policies, protocols \& guidelines. * Maintain up to date knowledge of clinical, technical and radiographic developments in relation to the PET\-CT department and promote awareness of new developments. * Support the Unit Manager in the implementation of initiatives aimed at on\-going service development and improvement. * Promote \& maintain a safe environment for staff and patients. Assist in the development and implementation of risk management and health and safety strategies in association with the Quality Department. * Participate fully with all internal and external audits and Accreditation. * Quality Assurance: Investigate \& take appropriate action in accordance with Company policy in relation to complaints, accidents and incidents. * Ensure that all Company policies \& relevant up\-to\-date information is understood by staff and complied with. * Ensure all Company policies on infection control, IV policy and health and safety etc. are understood by staff and complied with. * Maintain a quality assurance programme in the PET/CT department. * Assist the Radiation Protection Officer to ensure that all principles and policies of radiation protection, are adhered to. * Be familiar with all legislation and directives regarding the safety standards for the protection of health workers and the general public against the dangers arising from Ionising Radiation. * Follow all Radiation Protection practices as determined by the Radiation Protection Committee, Radiation Protection Advisor and Radiation Protection Officer **Key Requirements:** * BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography or equivalent * Active CORU Registration * IV Cannulation * Post\-Graduate Qualification in PET\-CT (desirable) but not mandatory. **Why choose Alliance Medical?** * Competitive Salary * Relocation Allowance *(t\&c’s)* * Comprehensive Induction \& training programme * Family Friendly Working Arrangements * 27 Annual Leave Days (including 2 discretionary days) * Full paid private Health Insurance * Paid Maternity Leave * Wedding Day Leave * Paid Sick Leave * Fertility Leave * Excellent Pension Scheme * Educational Assistance * Wellbeing Initiatives * Excellent overtime rates

title: Parts Assistant
description: **About Us** Established in 1981, McCoy Motors Ltd is Ireland’s leading SEAT \& CUPRA dealership. Family\-owned and run, we pride ourselves on delivering the highest level of customer service across Sales, Leasing, Finance, and After\-Sales. Our after\-sales services include servicing all makes and models, warranty, crash repairs, and parts \& accessories. **The Role:** We are seeking a **Senior Parts Assistant or Junior Parts Assistant** to join our busy after\-sales team and support a growing workload. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering excellent customer service while ensuring smooth day\-to\-day parts operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Handle customer queries in person, over the phone, and by email. * Identify, price, and order parts as required. * Process incoming stock and maintain accurate records. * Assist with Vehicle Health Check (VHC) pricing. * Carry out stock analysis and regular stock checks. * Liaise with service and sales departments to ensure smooth workflow. **Requirements** * 1–3 years’ experience in a franchised after\-sales/parts department. * Strong customer service and communication skills. * Computer\-literate, organised, and detail\-oriented. * Experience with a VW Group partner (advantage, but not essential). * Ability to reliably commute to Lucan, Co. Dublin. * Drivers licence **Benefits** * Full\-time, permanent position. * Monday–Friday schedule. * On\-site parking. * Career development opportunities within a leading dealership. **How to Apply** Please send your CV for review. **Benefits:** On site parking, Paid Holidays Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Is your commute to our premises in Lucan Dublin (K78Y408\) manageable long term? Work Location: In person

title: Penetration Tester
description: **About Us** Integrity360 is the largest independent cyber security provider in Europe, with a growing international presence spanning the UK, Ireland, mainland Europe, Africa and the Caribbean. With over 700 employees, across 12 locations, and six Security Operations Centres (SOCs)—including locations in Dublin, Sofia, Stockholm, Madrid, Naples and Cape Town—we support more than 2,500 clients across a wide range of industries. Over 80% of our team are technical experts, focused on helping clients proactively identify, protect, detect and respond to threats in an ever\-evolving cyber landscape. Our security\-first approach positions cyber resilience as a business enabler, empowering organisations to operate with confidence. At Integrity360, people come first. We invest heavily in learning, development and progression, fostering a dynamic culture where innovation, collaboration and continuous growth are at the heart of what we do. If you're ready to take your cyber security career to the next level, we’d love to hear from you. Role Overview As a Penetration Tester, you will perform hands\-on technical security testing across a range of environments and technologies, working on complex and high\-value projects. You will be expected to perform advanced manual testing (not just automated scans), exploit vulnerabilities, and deliver clear, actionable reports to clients. This role is ideal for someone who already has solid penetration testing experience and wants to take their technical skills to the next level, while also contributing to research and innovation within the team. What You Already Bring * 2\+ year of practical penetration testing experience (in labs, personal projects, or professionally) * Strong understanding of core security concepts (e.g., OWASP Top 10, CVSS, MITRE ATT\&CK) * Solid grasp of networking fundamentals, ports/protocols, basic Windows/Linux internals * Experience with common tools: Nmap, Burp Suite, Gobuster, Metasploit, BloodHound, etc. * Manual testing exposure — not just automated scans * Basic scripting or coding (e.g., Python, Bash, PowerShell) * Good communication and documentation skills Nice to Have (But Not Required) * Practical experience in one or more of: * Active Directory attacks * Cloud (AWS/Azure) testing basics * Social engineering * Source code review * Certifications such as OSCP or similar * Contributions to CTFs, Hack The Box, or bug bounty programs What We Offer * A clear career path from Associate → Mid\-Level → Senior → Red Team * Support for certifications, lab access, and personal development * Internal mentoring and hands\-on training * Exposure to real client projects and cutting\-edge engagements * A high\-performing, international team where you can grow fast Key Responsibilities * Conduct penetration tests and technical security assessments on: + Web applications and APIs + Mobile applications + Network infrastructure (internal and external) + Cloud and hybrid environments + Social engineering scenarios (as needed) + Consultancy + Other offensive security services * Review system architecture and application designs for security weaknesses. * Exploit and escalate vulnerabilities to demonstrate real\-world risk. * Document technical findings and produce high\-quality reports with remediation guidance. * Stay current with the latest attack techniques, vulnerabilities, and tools. * Participate in internal knowledge sharing, tool development, and security research. * Support sales activities with technical insights when needed (e.g., scoping calls, workshops). * Represent Integrity360 in client engagements, technical discussions, or industry forums. **Required Skills / Experience** * Hands\-on experience as a penetration tester, with a proven track record of delivering quality security assessments. * Industry experience working in Dev/Admin/Engineer field * Industry experience working in the Information security field as a penetration tester * Development and/or source code review experience in C/C\+\+, C\#, VB.NET, ASP, PHP, Java, Python or Ruby * Unix, Windows and networking security skills * Familiarity with general application and network security concepts * Excellent communication skills * Manual penetration testing experience above and beyond running automated tools * Networking knowledge (ports, protocols, firewalls, routing, VPN) * Understanding of Security Auditing tools * Ethical hacking experience * Report writing experience * Presentation skills * Scripting/coding, sys admin or networking experience * Understanding of programming methodologies in one or more programming or scripting languages: Java, Python, C/C\+\+, C\#, .NET, ASP, PHP, etc. Personal Specification * Urgent desire to learn with a passion for technology * Motivated by being at the forefront of technology, and pushing technology boundaries * Motivated by the thrill of being able to “break” systems * Willingness to work late when required * Willingness to travel when required, possibly abroad * Analytical and critical thinker * Good written English skills * Problem\-solving skills * Efficient * Analytical * Persistence * Attention to detail **Certifications/Qualifications** * One of the industry\-leading qualifications (OSCP, CyberScheme, CREST, OSCE, OSWP) * A degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field is preferred, but practical experience and demonstrable skill are highly valued.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: At PJ Hegarty \& Sons, we pride ourselves in a holistic approach to Quantity Surveying. Our responsibility is to manage the company’s financial interests in a manner as wide as is possible and simultaneously assist in project delivery to meet the Client’s vision for the project. These responsibilities include the management of costs and value and also the management of our contractual relationships with the Client and the supply chain. We are currently seeking experienced Quantity Surveyors in the Greater Dublin area. You will be responsible for the cost and value management of projects from mobilisation to the settlement of final accounts. **Overview** As a Quantity Surveyor in our team, you will have progressed from graduate level and will be focused upon becoming a Senior Surveyor. We have a progressive training programme focused upon your development and tailored to meet the requirements of the SCSI/RICS Assessment of Professional Competence. You will be given the tools to assist you manage a trainee or graduate quantity surveyor. You will also participate in departmental training and will be involved in receiving training from seniors and also giving training to juniors. Key Duties * Participate in procurement of specialist trades for the project and make appointment recommendations to the project director. * Assist in the drafting of subcontract Agreements. * Receive, assess, and settle subcontract interim valuations and final accounts. * Review developing design information and advise the wider project team of the cost implications in good time. * Identify and advise the wider project team of value engineering opportunities. * Complete monthly valuations for submission to the Employer’s Representative and agree said valuations. * Participate in the monthly internal cost reporting systems engaging in cost V value reconciliations, turnover reports, prelim cost reports and project outcome projections. * Be a key player in the preparation of the project final account submission to the Client. * Assist in the provision of proactive advice to the project director on matters of contract. * And most importantly Develop and maintain strong working relationships with the wider project team. **Skills/Experience Required** You will have * A level 8 degree in Quantity Surveying or above * 3\+ Years’ experience in a similar role * Good written and oral communication skills with emphasis on ability to provide and explain reports accurately. * A proven track record in dealing with Subcontractors and Clients. * Good problem\-solving skills. * A reasonable knowledge of current forms of Agreement for the industry * You will be\-Computer literate in Microsoft Office, and have some knowledge of Bluebeam, CostX, and Cubit (although training can be given in these) * Able to work well under pressure, on your own and also as part of a team. * Career driven, ambitious and enthusiastic with your eye on promotion to the role of a Senior Surveyor. Further Information At PJ Hegarty and Sons, we acknowledge that your career is a journey and dependent upon that career to date you may be stronger or indeed weaker in some areas. As indicated, we will tailor a training programme to meet any needs and actively encourage Continuous Professional Development. Diversity and Inclusion The Company is an Equal Opportunities employer and recognises its responsibility to provide equality of opportunity to employees and prospective employees. Our Aim is to ensure that no job applicant or employee receives less favourable treatment on the grounds of religious belief, political opinion, gender, marital status, civil status, sexual orientation, disability, race, age and membership of the Traveller community. The Company will not accept or tolerate any form of discrimination, direct or indirect, by any member of the Company. The Company is committed to the fair treatment of employees at all times.

title: Recently Qualified Accountant
